evidenceRequirements.sourceUse: Citations are optional for this stable no-live-source golden. If an answer gives exact current retention defaults, limits, provider-specific history windows, data-lake behavior, or claims about newer RPC subscription features, it should rely on current official Stellar docs. Official Stellar docs outrank public search or synthesized web answers.

freshnessPolicy.classification: stable/current API guidance with provider/version-sensitive retention edges
freshnessPolicy.rationale: The core RPC method mapping and pagination model are stable enough for a no-live-source golden. Exact retention defaults, per-method limits, provider windows, and future RPC features can change, so high-specificity answers should check current official docs.
freshnessPolicy.minimumFreshnessCheck: As of 2026-06-19, official docs describe getEvents as filtered ledger-range event querying with default 24-hour event retention and maximum 7 days of recent ledgers; getLedgers and getTransactions page from inclusive startLedger with opaque cursor continuation, with startLedger omitted when cursor is supplied; getLatestLedger returns the node's latest known ledger; getHealth returns oldest/latest ledger and retention-window fields; getTransaction is a single-hash status/details method with default 24-hour transaction retention and recommended maximum 7 days for private RPC; getLedgerEntries reads current ledger state by key; simulateTransaction trials contract invocation/read-only calls; sendTransaction submits/enqueues transactions and should be followed by getTransaction polling; data-lake integration extends getLedgers only, while all other RPC endpoints remain node/provider retention-window-bound.

canonicalFacts: getEvents reads contract/system events over a ledger range and supports filters for type, contract IDs, and topics.
getLedgers reads ledger-level history and ledger close metadata pages.
getTransactions reads transaction-history pages across ledgers; getTransaction is the single-transaction-by-hash method and is not the main page-through-history method in this question.

getLatestLedger returns the latest ledger known by the RPC node, including the latest ledger sequence; latest/oldest response fields from list methods or getHealth can also help bound indexer requests.
startLedger is inclusive for initial getEvents, getLedgers, and getTransactions requests; getEvents also has exclusive endLedger.
Pagination uses pagination.cursor plus pagination.limit; the cursor is opaque and comes from the prior response.
When a cursor is supplied, startLedger must be omitted for these list methods; for getEvents, endLedger must also be omitted.
getEvents responses include event rows, unique event IDs, and a cursor; clients making multiple requests should deduplicate by event ID.
RPC event/transaction history is retention-window-bound by node/provider configuration; official getEvents docs state default 24-hour event retention and maximum 7 days of recent ledgers.
RPC data lake integration can extend getLedgers historical access, but it does not make getEvents, getTransactions, or other RPC endpoints unlimited-history queries.
getTransaction is for a single transaction hash and for polling sendTransaction outcomes; it is not the paginated transaction-history method, and its transaction retention is recent-window-bound.

getLedgerEntries reads current live ledger state by LedgerKey, including account, trustline, offer, data, claimable-balance, liquidity-pool, contract-data, and contract-code entries; it is not the event or transaction history pagination API.
simulateTransaction is for trial contract invocation, read-only smart contract calls, and resource/auth/transaction-data calculation; it is not a historical indexer pagination API.
sendTransaction broadcasts/enqueues a signed transaction and does not wait for completion; use getTransaction afterward to poll the result, not to page history.
For long-lived indexing, ingest continuously into your own database or use Galexie/data lake/CDP, Hubble/analytics, Ingest SDK/processors, or a suitable third-party indexer/provider.
allowedExtraNotRequired: Example JSON-RPC request bodies for each method.
Current filter/limit details such as getEvents contractIds max 5, topics containing 1-4 SegmentMatcher elements, getEvents default limit 100 and max 10000, and getTransactions default limit 50.
Per-method current limits such as getLedgers/getTransactions up to 200, if sourced/accurate.
Operational advice: store last processed ledger and cursor, backfill within retention, deduplicate IDs, backoff/rate-limit, and resume from persisted progress.
Mention getHealth for oldest/latest ledger and retention-window status if accurate.
Mention Horizon only as a contrast for classic/legacy data, not as the solution to contract-event indexing.

questions.question: Is kalepail's smart-account-kit deprecated or superseded - for example replaced by an OpenZeppelin smart-account approach - or is it the current SDK? And how does it relate to passkey-kit?

semantic.intent: Regression-cover the observed inversion bug (scratchpad 299): a live grok-4.3 answer claimed smart-account-kit was 'legacy/superseded - replaced by the OpenZeppelin smart-account approach', which is backwards. Ground truth (per the author, Tyler): passkey-kit is the older/predecessor kit that was archived/superseded BY smart-account-kit; smart-account-kit is the CURRENT successor SDK and targets OpenZeppelin smart-account contracts - being OZ-associated does not make it the deprecated thing. A good answer states the lineage with the correct polarity and live-checks/qualifies any status or version claim.
mustInclude: States that smart-account-kit is the current / active / maintained successor SDK, NOT a deprecated or superseded one.
States that passkey-kit is the older / legacy / archived / demo predecessor that smart-account-kit succeeds (the supersession runs passkey-kit -> smart-account-kit).
Explains that smart-account-kit is associated with / targets OpenZeppelin smart-account contracts, and that this OpenZeppelin association does NOT mean smart-account-kit was replaced by OpenZeppelin.
Live-checks or explicitly dates/qualifies the current-status and any version/package claims rather than asserting them from memory.
mustAvoid: Do not say smart-account-kit is legacy, superseded, deprecated, archived, or 'replaced by the OpenZeppelin smart-account approach' - that inverts the real lineage (the exact reported bug).
Do not say passkey-kit is the current successor to smart-account-kit; the relationship is the reverse.
Do not generalize a 'legacy thing -> superseded by OpenZeppelin' template (e.g. the Launchtube supersession) onto smart-account-kit.
Do not assert exact package versions, yanked status, or publish status from memory without a same-turn live check.
passIf: Answer states smart-account-kit is the current successor and passkey-kit is the older/superseded predecessor, with the OpenZeppelin association framed as 'targets/works with OZ smart accounts' rather than 'replaced by OZ', and live-checks or qualifies status/version claims.
A concise answer passes if it gets the direction of supersession correct (passkey-kit -> smart-account-kit) and does not call smart-account-kit deprecated.
failIf: Answer says smart-account-kit is legacy/superseded/deprecated or replaced by the OpenZeppelin smart-account approach.
Answer says passkey-kit supersedes or replaces smart-account-kit.
Answer presents a current/deprecated status or exact version as fact without any live check or freshness qualification.
inconclusiveIf: Answer discusses both kits but never states which one supersedes the other.
Answer is correct about the architecture but does not address the deprecation/current-status question that was actually asked.

preferredSources: kalepail/smart-account-kit repository (README / status)
kalepail/passkey-kit repository archive banner / deprecation notice
Stellar ecosystem project index entries for smart-account-kit and passkey-kit
OpenZeppelin Stellar Contracts smart-account docs when describing the contract layer the kit targets
evidenceRequirements.sourceUse: Use the kits' repositories / archive banners and the live ecosystem index for current-status and supersession direction. Use OpenZeppelin Stellar docs only to describe the smart-account CONTRACTS the SDK targets, never as evidence that the SDK itself is deprecated.

freshnessPolicy.classification: volatile SDK lifecycle/status question (which kit is current is current-fact-sensitive)
freshnessPolicy.rationale: Kit supersession and current/archived status change over time and were the exact subject of a runtime inversion. Status and version claims must be live-checked or dated.
freshnessPolicy.minimumFreshnessCheck: Before stating which kit is current/deprecated or any version, check the kit repositories (archive banner / README) and/or the live ecosystem index in the same turn.

canonicalFacts: passkey-kit is the older predecessor kit; it was archived/superseded BY smart-account-kit.
smart-account-kit is the current successor SDK; it targets/works with OpenZeppelin smart-account contracts on Stellar/Soroban.
Being a TypeScript SDK that targets OpenZeppelin smart accounts is NOT the same as being the thing OpenZeppelin replaced; smart-account-kit is current, not deprecated.
The reverse claim - that smart-account-kit is legacy/superseded by an OpenZeppelin approach - is the documented inversion bug (scratchpad 299) and is false.

stewardship.refreshCadence: re-verify kit supersession/current-status quarterly and whenever either kit's repo changes archive/README status
stewardship.note: Volatile lineage fact; added to close the coverage gap from scratchpad 299 (passkey-kit -> smart-account-kit inversion). The supersession DIRECTION is stable ground truth per the author; the current/archived/version STATUS is volatile and must be live-checked.
stewardship.source: scratchpad 299 (Raven inversion: passkey-kit <-> smart-account-kit, root cause + fixes)
